content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements to ensure __exit__ is called and file handles are closed.

fields.note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements to ensure __exit__ is called and file handles are closed.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py:_resolve_context() body,src/click/core.py:exit() docstring only (lines ~703-709)
your_files: src/click/core.py:Context class (validate_nesting param/init/close/scope logic),src/click/shell_completion.py: single kwarg line inside _resolve_context
note: Both our features touch the same 2 files, so splitting by region not file. I own: full with-wrapping rewrite of _resolve_context() in shell_completion.py (lines ~532-586), plus a tiny isolated docstring-only edit to Context.exit() in core.py (~line 706-708, just changes wording of a versionchanged note, no code logic). You own: all Context class functional changes in core.py (validate_nesting param, __init__, close(), scope(), RuntimeError on leak). For your shell_completion.py need: after I land my _resolve_context rewrite, the first line of that function stays 'ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True' unchanged - just add your 'ctx_args["validate_nesting"] = False' (or similar) line immediately after that, before the 'with cli.make_context(...)' line. That's the only shell_completion.py line you need. Confirm and I'll proceed to implement.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed: I own shell_completion.py exclusively (with-statement rewrite of _resolve_context, and I'll add validate_nesting=False to all 3 make_context calls inside it: the top-level cli.make_context, the non-chained cmd.make_context, and the chained cmd.make_context). You own core.py exclusively for the Context.validate_nesting/close/scope changes. Dropping my earlier core.py docstring tweak idea - not essential. Proceeding to implement now.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: My _resolve_context with-statement rewrite is landed, tested (existing shell completion tests pass, and verified no ResourceWarning is raised with python -W error::ResourceWarning on a click.File-using command completion). Patch written to /workspace/repo/patch.txt. You're clear to add your validate_nesting=False line(s) in shell_completion.py now - the 3 make_context call sites are: (1) 'with cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args) as ctx:' near original line 547, (2) 'with cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True) as sub_ctx:' for non-chained groups, (3) 'with cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, allow_extra_args=True, allow_interspersed_args=False, resilient_parsing=True) as sub_sub_ctx:' for chained groups. I'm not touching core.py at all - that's fully yours.
